name: ConvertFrom-Json

options.name: -AsHashtable
options.description: Converts the JSON to a hash table object. This switch was introduced in PowerShell 6.0. Starting with PowerShell 7.3, the object is an OrderedHashtable and preserves the ordering of the keys from the JSON. In prior versions, the object is a Hashtable .

options.name: -Depth
options.description: Gets or sets the maximum depth the JSON input is allowed to have. The default is 1024.

options.name: -InputObject
options.description: Specifies the JSON strings to convert to JSON objects. Enter a variable that contains the string, or type a command or expression that gets the string. You can also pipe a string to `ConvertFrom-Json`.

args.name: -InputObject
args.description: Specifies the JSON strings to convert to JSON objects. Enter a variable that contains the string, or type a command or expression that gets the string. You can also pipe a string to `ConvertFrom-Json`.

description: Converts a JSON-formatted string to a custom object or a hash table.
